trials.reason: Book B groups all backend HistoryService/query logic together, then all frontend consumers (ActivityFilter, ActivityView, ActivityItem) in dependency order, whereas Book A interleaves backend and frontend tests/chunks in a way that revisits HistoryService.cs repeatedly out of logical order.

Book A groups each file's full set of changes into one section (e.g. all of HistoryService.cs's methods together, all of ActivityFilter.svelte's script/template together), so related code is read as one coherent unit, whereas Book B fragments the same file into dozens of scattered sections interleaved with unrelated files, forcing constant context-switching.

Book A groups whole files/features into 32 coherent sections (e.g., all of HistoryService.cs's related methods together, all ActivityFilter.svelte pieces together), while Book B fragments the same file into 76 tiny interleaved sections that force jumping back and forth between unrelated files before completing one concern.

metadata.note: single-family judge (Claude): self-preference caveat applies; generator/judge ids must differ where possible
